parts.text: I am Kunal, and I once kept something that glittered too—one lie I told myself was harmless—and I still flinch when I touch that place inside my chest where it lives. Tanha dressed as fairness, upadana dressed as justice; modern minds call it cognitive rationalization, but the Buddha saw it three millennia earlier: the mind manufactures reasons the way the tongue manufactures saliva, automatically, endlessly, to swallow whatever it craves.
